author: Dr. Rebecca Yang (Neurology — neuromuscular)
content:
ELECTROMYOGRAPHY AND NERVE CONDUCTION STUDY — HOSPITAL DAY 4
Date/Time: 3/18/2027, 3:30 PM
Patient: Sandoval, Maria Isabel (MRN: MRN-90215734)
Referring service: Internal Medicine / Rheumatology
Location: Bedside (patient too weak for transport)
Interpreter: Spanish phone interpreter present.

INDICATION:
Proximal weakness pattern with elevated muscle enzymes (CK 2580, aldolase 28) in setting of active SLE flare. EMG/NCS to characterize the pattern, identify a target muscle for biopsy, and exclude superimposed neuropathy.

PATIENT INFORMATION:
23-year-old woman with severely malnourished body habitus (BMI 15.7), generalized weakness, dysphagia, dysphonia. Anasarca present. Awake, cooperative.

TECHNIQUE:
NCS performed using standard surface electrodes. EMG performed using disposable monopolar needles. Sensory and motor NCS limited to upper and lower extremities by examination time and patient tolerance. EMG focused on representative proximal and distal muscles including deltoid, biceps brachii, vastus lateralis, tibialis anterior, gastrocnemius, and right paraspinal at L4 level. Limb temperatures normalized.

NERVE CONDUCTION STUDIES:

Motor NCS:
- Right median (recording APB): Distal latency 3.4 ms (normal), CMAP amplitude 6.8 mV (normal >4), conduction velocity 52 m/s (normal).
- Right ulnar (recording ADM): DL 2.8 ms, CMAP 8.2 mV, CV 56 m/s — all normal.
- Right peroneal (recording EDB): DL 4.2 ms, CMAP 4.6 mV (normal >2), CV 46 m/s — all normal.
- Right tibial (recording AH): DL 4.8 ms, CMAP 12.4 mV, CV 48 m/s — all normal.
- Right facial (recording orbicularis oculi): DL 3.0 ms, CMAP 2.4 mV — normal.

Sensory NCS:
- Right median (digit 2): SNAP amplitude 28 uV (normal), CV 56 m/s.
- Right ulnar (digit 5): SNAP 18 uV, CV 54 m/s.
- Right sural: SNAP 14 uV, CV 48 m/s.
All normal.

Repetitive nerve stimulation (right ulnar at 3 Hz, recording ADM): No significant decrement (<3%); ruled out myasthenia gravis.

F-waves: present and within normal range bilaterally.
H-reflex: present and symmetric.

MOTOR NCS INTERPRETATION: NORMAL.
SENSORY NCS INTERPRETATION: NORMAL.
No evidence of peripheral neuropathy. No evidence of neuromuscular junction disorder (negative RNS).

ELECTROMYOGRAPHY (NEEDLE EMG):

Right deltoid (proximal upper extremity):
- Insertional activity: INCREASED.
- Spontaneous activity: FIBRILLATIONS 2+ and POSITIVE SHARP WAVES 2+ at rest. No fasciculations. No myotonic discharges.
- Motor unit potentials (MUPs): Decreased duration (mean 6.2 ms, normal 9-15 ms), decreased amplitude (mean 360 uV, normal 500-2000), increased polyphasia (38%, normal <15%). EARLY RECRUITMENT pattern (small MUPs firing rapidly at low force).

Right biceps brachii:
- Insertional activity: INCREASED.
- Spontaneous activity: Fibrillations 2+ and PSWs 2+.
- MUPs: Short, small, polyphasic with early recruitment — similar to deltoid.

Right vastus lateralis:
- Insertional activity: INCREASED.
- Spontaneous activity: Fibrillations 3+, PSWs 3+ (most florid in vastus lateralis).
- MUPs: Short duration, low amplitude, polyphasic. Early recruitment pronounced.
- (THIS MUSCLE IDENTIFIED AS THE PRIMARY BIOPSY TARGET.)

Right tibialis anterior:
- Insertional activity: Mildly increased.
- Spontaneous activity: Occasional fibrillations 1+, rare PSWs.
- MUPs: Mildly short, polyphasic. Mostly preserved recruitment.

Right gastrocnemius (medial head):
- Mild changes: insertional activity normal, no fibrillations, MUPs mildly polyphasic. Largely preserved.

Right paraspinals (L4 level):
- Insertional activity: INCREASED.
- Spontaneous activity: Fibrillations 2+.
- MUPs: Short duration polyphasic.

Distal lower extremity (gastrocnemius) and distal upper extremity were less affected than proximal muscles — consistent with a PROXIMAL > DISTAL pattern.

EMG INTERPRETATION:
Abnormal study with findings of an ACUTE / SUBACUTE MYOPATHIC PROCESS with active denervation features (fibrillations, positive sharp waves) and small-amplitude, short-duration, polyphasic MUPs with early recruitment. The pattern is symmetric, proximal-predominant, with paraspinal involvement.

DIFFERENTIAL CONSIDERATIONS:
The combination of (1) active myopathic changes, (2) prominent spontaneous activity (fibrillations + PSWs), (3) proximal symmetric distribution with paraspinal involvement, and (4) early recruitment with small MUPs is HIGHLY SUGGESTIVE of an INFLAMMATORY MYOPATHY (polymyositis, dermatomyositis, immune-mediated necrotizing myopathy, or SLE-associated myositis). Less consistent with:
- Toxic / drug-induced myopathy (typically less spontaneous activity)
- Endocrine myopathy / hypothyroid myopathy (typically less spontaneous activity, though her TSH is elevated)
- Hereditary myopathy (less spontaneous activity)
- Inclusion body myositis (typically older, more distal/asymmetric)
- Steroid myopathy (typically NO spontaneous activity)

The ABSENCE of myotonic discharges argues against myotonic dystrophy.
The ABSENCE of decrement on RNS argues against MG.
The NORMAL sensory NCS rules out a sensorimotor neuropathy.

IMPRESSION:
1. ELECTROPHYSIOLOGIC EVIDENCE OF AN ACTIVE INFLAMMATORY MYOPATHY, proximal-predominant, with paraspinal involvement.
2. No evidence of peripheral neuropathy.
3. No evidence of neuromuscular junction disorder (MG ruled out by RNS).

RECOMMENDED BIOPSY TARGET: RIGHT VASTUS LATERALIS — most florid spontaneous activity and pathognomonic MUP changes; ideal for histopathologic confirmation. Avoid distal muscles for biopsy as they are less involved.

Biopsy target communicated directly to rheumatology and to neurosurgery / IR team for biopsy planning HD5 (3/19/2027).

Discussed with rheumatology (Dr. Park) and primary medicine team (Dr. Castro). Patient and mother also informed via interpreter that the EMG confirms the muscle inflammation pattern and that biopsy tomorrow will provide the definitive diagnosis.

Study completed at bedside in ~85 minutes. Patient tolerated reasonably well despite weakness; she dozed at several points during the study. Total interpreter time during study: 30 minutes for explanation and consent.
attestation:
Electronically signed by:
Dr. Rebecca Yang, MD (Neuromuscular Neurology, Attending)
Certified electromyographer, AANEM
